failure_driver_assessment.evidence: 48 bounded-harm metric comparisons versus 10 non-worsening comparisons.
Bounded harm appears in travel-time/delay metrics, so objective tuning remains a plausible route.
Finite-storage decisions differed from pressure in 9.833% of audited TLS decisions; nonzero components: {'pressure': 3338, 'switching': 745}.
221 metric comparisons were inconclusive, so scenario or metric sensitivity remains unresolved.
Only 4 strict-positive signals were present against strong max-pressure-style comparators.

claim_boundary.rule: v1.3 failed Gate C; diagnostics and v1.4 pilots cannot be imported as final superiority evidence.

workstreams.scope: Change the live controller score decomposition while preserving auditable action components.
Retune objective component weights and penalties without changing the confirmation protocol after lock.
Audit whether scenario design or binding activation explains the v1.3 failure.
Test whether a compressed symbolic policy can keep auditability while improving action choices.

selection_criteria.description: Pilot must reduce bounded-harm counts or show no new bounded harm against strong baselines.
Strict-positive signals are useful only when paired with non-worsening primary metrics.
Action decomposition must show finite-storage/spillback/switching terms changing decisions.
Pilot artifacts must expose score/objective terms and action decomposition.
Candidate must have deterministic command, inputs, and artifact paths.
